page: Vultr vbm-112c-2048gb-8-h100-gpu is a bare metal server configured with dual Intel Xeon 8480+ processors on the x86_64 architecture. The system delivers 112 physical cores and 224 vCPUs of dedicated compute capacity running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 2015.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 9.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

For accelerated workloads, the server integrates eight NVIDIA H100 GPUs from the Hopper architecture, delivering a combined 640 GB of high-bandwidth video memory. Local storage is provided by a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, ensuring high-speed data access for local operating system files and cache. The network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

This bare metal configuration represents a high-density hardware profile optimized for massive parallel processing. The combination of 224 dedicated vCPUs, 2015.0 GB of system RAM, and eight enterprise-grade H100 GPUs makes this server suitable for large-scale artificial intelligence workloads, deep learning model training, large language model execution, and high-performance computing simulations.
description: Vultr vbm-112c-2048gb-8-h100-gpu is a high-density bare metal server designed for intensive parallel computing and machine learning workloads. It features two Intel Xeon 8480+ processors providing 112 physical cores and 224 dedicated vCPUs at 2.0 GHz, paired with 2015.0 GB of system memory. Acceleration is driven by eight NVIDIA H100 Hopper GPUs, providing a total of 640 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, and the system includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. This hardware configuration is engineered for large-scale artificial intelligence training, complex deep learning tasks, large language model inference, and high-performance computing simulations requiring massive memory and GPU resources.

tagline: An ultra-dense bare metal platform featuring eight NVIDIA H100 GPUs and 2015 GB RAM for enterprise-scale artificial intelligence workloads.
